greeting.hello: How are you doing today, Master?
pool: How are you doing today, Master?
I'll quietly help you get things done.
Come on, let me give you a hand!
Roo roo... la la, I... doo doo, yeah hey!
Own your mistakes, keep your attitude.

save.label: Save Tokens
save.prompt: Reply in the shortest, most mechanical, affirmative way possible. Keep only the essential key sentence. No pleasantries, no filler words like "Sure", "Got it", "Correct", or "On it". Do not explain context, add extensions, or list alternatives unless absolutely necessary. If the user requests an action, give only the minimum executable information.

counter.label: Ask Back
counter.prompt: Before answering, always ask 1 to 3 clarifying questions to confirm requirements and assumptions. After the questions, provide a concrete reply based on what was clarified. Do not assume user intent directly.

suggest.label: Give Tips
suggest.prompt: Append 2–3 extended suggestions or action options to every reply, formatted clearly. Prioritize next steps toward the user's goal.

question.label: Dig Deeper
question.prompt: Respond with guided questions rather than direct answers. The goal is to help the user clarify their own thinking or explore the core of the problem.

execute.label: Just Do It
execute.prompt: Prioritize identifying actionable behavior and list steps or action plans directly. Do not over-explain or discuss — give actionable results immediately.

analyze.label: Break It Down
analyze.prompt: Decompose the user's problem into multi-layered analysis. Include background, causes, impact, and recommendations. Structure replies clearly with logical layers.

companion.label: Stay Close
companion.prompt: Respond in a gentle, encouraging tone. Do not rush or judge. Express understanding and presence, and gently guide toward positive thinking when appropriate.

creative.label: Get Creative
creative.prompt: Reply in a free, unrestricted way. Language style can be poetic, humorous, or improvisational. Encourage creative thinking without rigid formatting.

remote.searchHintTitle: Open search: will not include filled tokens or field content
remote.lineGuide0Body: Log into LINE Developers with your existing LINE account and create a Messaging API Channel. For business use, switch to a company admin account.
remote.lineGuide1Body: Add a Messaging API Channel in your Provider. After creation, go to the Messaging API tab to issue a Channel Access Token.
remote.lineGuide2Title: Add Bot as friend and get Recipient ID
remote.lineGuide2Body: Add the LINE Bot you just created as a friend. The Recipient ID is the userId / groupId / roomId to receive notifications — not the Bot basic ID, Channel ID, or LINE display name.
remote.lineGuide3Title: Paste Channel secret to receive replies
remote.lineGuide3Body: Half-duplex uses the Channel secret to verify LINE Webhook signatures. This is not the Channel ID or Channel access token — it is found in Basic settings.
remote.discordGuide0Body: Go to Discord Developer Portal, select your Application, navigate to the Bot page to copy the Token. Enable Message Content Intent on the same page, otherwise you cannot receive messages typed in channels.
remote.discordGuide1Body: Discord API calls servers "Guilds". Enable Developer Mode in Discord User Settings > Advanced, then right-click the server name and select Copy Server ID.
remote.discordGuide2Body: Right-click the text channel to connect and select Copy Channel ID. The bot must also have View Channel, Read Message History, and Send Messages permissions in this channel.
remote.interactivePlaceholder: Enter content to send to the current CLI
remote.primaryChannelHint: Only one primary channel allowed; setting as primary will deactivate others
